name: oubliette
summary: A pit-prison — the forgotten place. One way in (above), bones on the floor, no hope of getting out.

primary_palette.note: generations of forgotten prisoners, bones heaped under the grate
recent addition — one still-fleshed body among older bones
a prisoner chained to the wall, long dead
the room's defining feature is the hole in the ceiling — nothing else, bare stone
working oubliette — multiple dead in various stages

story_prompts: centuries-old, bones packed two feet deep
occupied — a starving prisoner still alive at the bottom
empty except for a scratched message in a dead language
collapsed oubliette, a way out now that didn't exist before
a false oubliette — the bones are decoys, the floor is a trapdoor to worse

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: near-black (0.05-0.15), light only from the grate above

anti_patterns: don't furnish — no beds, no tables, no chairs. Oubliettes are bare.
don't put a door at floor level — the entry is above, via trapdoor or grate
don't light it well; this room is defined by its darkness
